news.summary: In Bangkok on May 16, 2025, during Expo 2025 in Osaka, Japan, the Thai Pavilion attracted more than 200,000 visitors in just one month. news.headline: Thai Pavilion at Expo 2025 in Osaka, Japan attracts 200,000 visitors within first month. blending innovation with tradition.

news.headline: Alpine Funded of Switzerland marks one year, grows globally as top trading firm, attracting traders worldwide.

news.summary: In preparation for upcoming busy shopping periods, the e-commerce industry is abuzz with the launch of Smart Push and Push Plan by EngageLab. The innovations come at a crucial time as businesses gear up for lucrative sales periods, with global e-commerce projected to reach a record $4.2 trillion in sales for the year. The introduction of these features is poised to revolutionize how companies approach customer interactions and promotions, promising to enhance user experiences and drive higher revenues in the competitive online marketplace.
news.headline: EngageLab enhances e-commerce with Smart Push and Push Plan, raising click-through rates by 30%.

news.summary: Yat Siu, chairman of Animoca Brands, stated at Consensus 2025 in Toronto that bringing student loans onchain could potentially increase the total value locked (TVL) in decentralized finance (DeFi) by over four times. With the global student loan market valued at $3 trillion, moving just 10% of it onchain could significantly boost DeFi's growth. Siu emphasized the potential for Web3-based financial tools to drive mass crypto adoption, particularly among students and the unbanked. He highlighted Animoca Brands' recent investment in Pencil Finance, a startup offering blockchain-backed student loans in regions like the Philippines and Indonesia, with plans to expand to the US. On April 30, Pencil Finance announced a $10 million student loan financing initiative. Siu emphasized the need for more positive-sum use cases in the industry that could make students more pro-crypto and suggested that integrating financial infrastructure into educational experiences could help form a new decentralized financial ecosystem. Siu also noted that education is a natural use case for Web3, and integrating financial tools into educational platforms could lead to the formation of capital assets from learning communities and reputational networks.
news.headline: Animoca's Yat Siu believes bringing student loans onchain could boost DeFi growth by 4x, tapping into $3 trillion global market, paving way for mass crypto adoption. Web3 tools for education sector also promising. Source highlights Animoca's investment in Pencil Finance, offering blockchain-backed student loans with plans for expansion. news.summary: XRP is currently retesting a falling wedge breakout, with a potential rally target of $3.60. The price has rebounded by over 50% in the last five weeks to reach $2.42, still 30% below its January 2025 peak of $3.40. A breakdown below $1.11 could trigger a sharp drop to $0.50, potentially indicating a bearish reversal. Whale wallets are currently in the red, with an average buy level of $2.58, suggesting a short-lived bounce followed by a broader pullback. If XRP rises towards the whale realized prices, it could reach $2.58 in the short term. However, in a worst-case scenario, the price could drop to $0.67. The article does not provide investment advice and advises readers to conduct their own research before making decisions.
news.headline: XRP retesting breakout, potential rally to $3.60. Whale wallets negative. Break below $1.11 could lead to drop to $0.50. XRP at $2.42 on May 16, 30% below peak. Risk of bearish reversal. Price drop to $0.67 possible. No investment advice. Conduct own research.

news.summary: Yat Siu, chairman of Animoca Brands, stated at Consensus 2025 in Toronto that bringing student loans onchain could increase DeFi TVL by over four times. He highlighted the $3 trillion global student loan market as a significant opportunity for the crypto industry. Moving just 10% of this market onchain could boost DeFi's growth significantly. Siu believes that Web3-based financial tools for education could drive mass crypto adoption, especially among the young and unbanked. Animoca Brands recently invested in Pencil Finance, a startup offering crypto-native student loans, with a $10 million initiative for cheaper, blockchain-backed loans. Siu emphasized that integrating financial infrastructure into educational experiences could turn learning communities into capital assets. This move could pave the way for a decentralized financial ecosystem. Siu sees education as a natural use case for Web3, comparing it to platforms like YouTube and TikTok which have transformed into significant learning platforms.
news.headline: Onchain student loans could skyrocket DeFi with $3 trillion market potential, says Animoca chairman at Consensus. Web3 tools drive future crypto adoption.

news.summary: US stock futures are trading with mixed sentiment this morning. Flowers Foods, Inc. (NYSE: FLO) is anticipated to announce quarterly earnings of 38 cents per share on revenue totaling $1.60 billion before the opening bell. In after-hours trading, Flowers Foods shares saw a 0.1% drop, resting at $17.05. On the other hand, Applied Materials Inc (NASDAQ: AMAT) posted second-quarter earnings that surpassed expectations, although sales fell short of estimates. Applied Materials forecasts third-quarter revenue to be around $7.2 billion, with a possible variance of $500 million. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: The CNN Money Fear and Greed index reflected a decrease in overall market sentiment, despite remaining in the "Greed" zone. On Thursday, U.S. stocks had a mixed settlement, with the S&P 500 marking gains for the fourth consecutive session. Throughout the week, the S&P 500 saw an increase of approximately 4.5%, while the Dow rose by about 2.6%. Over the same period, the Nasdaq surged by roughly 6.6%. Major tech companies experienced significant gains, with Nvidia and Tesla each adding around 15%. Amazon's shares climbed by about 6%, while Alphabet saw a 7% increase week-to-date. Walmart Inc. reported better-than-expected earnings for Q1 on Thursday. For further details, refer to the article available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: S&P 500 rises for fourth session with tech stocks boost. Fear index remains in 'Greed' zone. S&P gains 4.5%, Nasdaq 6.6%. Notable gains of Nvidia (NVDA) and Tesla (TSLA) at 15%, Amazon (AMZN) at 6%, and more. Walmart (WMT) reports strong earnings.

news.summary: In Singapore, Hafnia Limited ("Hafnia" or "Company"), with OSE ticker code "HAFNI" and NYSE ticker code “HAFN”, disclosed financial data for Q1 2025 on 15 May 2025. An irregularity was identified in the Q1 2025 Earnings Report on Page 6, involving the misrepresentation of broker valuations for the fleet. The report erroneously included 100% value of the joint venture vessels instead of the accurate 50%. This discrepancy has prompted a reevaluation of the financial information shared.
news.headline: Hafnia Limited corrects fleet value presentation in Q1 2025 report after including 100% instead of stated 50% joint venture vessels.

news.summary: The article outlines a bullish prediction for Bitcoin's price, suggesting it could reach $200,000 or higher this year, with potential to hit nearly $1 million by the end of the decade. The analysis is based on the interplay between Bitcoin and gold prices, with Bitcoin expected to follow gold to new all-time highs. The "power curve" model is used to measure Bitcoin's value in gold ounces, avoiding the inflationary nature of the US dollar. Additionally, if Bitcoin were to capture 50% of gold's market cap with gold hitting $5,000/oz by 2030, Bitcoin could potentially reach $924,000. The analysis also discusses Bitcoin's four-year price cycle and its potential for hitting $250,000 or more in 2025. The article emphasizes that all investment decisions involve risk, and readers should conduct their own research before acting.
news.headline: Bitcoin may hit $220K in 2025, with potential to reach $1 million by the decade's end, backed by gold-based forecast.
